titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 759

p.seg: David the earl to John the bishop, Colban, Cospatric, and all his sworn men sends greeting.

You yourselves know that there was a judgment before me between the monks of Saint Cuthbert and my drengs concerning the land of Horndean, that is to say, that if the monks had legal witnesses, or the brieve of my brother, the aforesaid land should remain theirs unchallenged.
For that reason I want you to know that I have seen the very brieve and gift of my brother, Edgar the king, which I have also sent to you.
And I will and grant that whatever that brieve bears witness in their favour I wish and allow that they have free and unchallenged.
As witness William my nephew, Osbert the chaplain, and Hugh de Morville.
